---
title: "Replication"
---

## Citation

> Prudencio, D. (2023). Productivity in procurement auctions of pavement contracts in Mexico. *Journal of Productivity Analysis*. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11123-025-00733-3

---

## BibTeX Entry

```bibtex
@article{prudencio2023procurement,
  author    = {Prudencio, Daniel},
  title     = {Productivity in procurement auctions of pavement contracts in {Mexico}},
  journal   = {Journal of Productivity Analysis},
  year      = {2023},
  publisher = {Springer},
  doi       = {10.1007/s11123-025-00733-3}
}
```

---

## Data and Code

### Data Sources

The analysis uses administrative procurement data from Mexico's **CompraNet** platform (Mexico's public procurement registry), covering pavement contracts across municipalities in Mexico from 2011 to 2018.

| Dataset | Description | Use |
|---------|-------------|-----|
| `bids_asym.csv` | Bid-level data with bid per m³ concrete (`fallo_concrete`) and firm type (`anydir_pav`) | Bid distribution chart in [Context & Data](context.qmd) |
| `data_xi.csv` | Pseudo-costs (`c_hat`) recovered from the structural model, by firm type (`typeb2`) | Pseudo-cost distribution chart in [Context & Data](context.qmd) |
| `data_densities.csv` | CDF of pseudo-costs at project size quantiles, simple contracts | Cost CDF chart in [Results](results.qmd) |
| `data_densities_drenaje.csv` | CDF of pseudo-costs at project size quantiles, complex contracts | Cost CDF chart in [Results](results.qmd) |
| `efficiency_index_comparison_drenaje_sfa.csv` | Firm-level BC efficiency index (`bc_h`) by type | Efficiency density chart in [Results](results.qmd) |
| `efficiency_index_sfa_drenaje.csv` | Firm-level BC index with state and municipality identifiers | State and municipality maps in [Results](results.qmd) |
| `panel_variables.csv` | Contract-level panel with municipality names and INEGI codes | Municipality–name lookup for maps |

---

### Methodological Code

The paper uses three separate computational environments, each for a different stage of the analysis:

**Stage 1: Structural auction model (Julia)**

The nonparametric GPV estimation, including kernel density estimation of bid distributions and recovery of pseudo-costs via the inversion formula, is implemented in **Julia**. Julia's performance is well-suited to the computationally intensive kernel bandwidth selection and cross-validation steps. The output is the pseudo-cost dataset (`data_xi.csv`, `data_densities.csv`, `data_densities_drenaje.csv`).

**Stage 2: Stochastic frontier analysis (Julia/Stata)**

The SFA models (half-normal and truncated-normal) are estimated via maximum likelihood. The efficiency index datasets (`efficiency_index_*.csv`) are the output of this stage.

**Stage 3: Figures in the paper (R)**

All figures published in the *Journal of Productivity Analysis* article were produced in **R** using `ggplot2`. The R scripts that generate the published figures are available in the project's `code_jmp/Exploratory R/` directory.

**This website (Python)**

The interactive charts on this website are independent reproductions of the paper's figures using **Python** (`plotly`, `scipy`, `pandas`). They draw on the same CSV output files as the R scripts, so the underlying data is identical, only the rendering tool differs.

---

### About This Website

This interactive summary was built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org), a scientific publishing system that renders Markdown and Python code into HTML. The source files for this website are available in the same GitHub repository as the paper's replication code.
